passages.passage_text: Directions: We come across many funny incidents related to different mishits. One of the funny incidents is the punchline or a climax - which gives the incident a sudden transformation or twist (into something not expected). It is this punchline which makes the incident funny. Based on the following questions, an incident is described but the punchline line is hidden by a blank. After the incident, two statements numbered I and II are given. Considering the incident, you have to decide which of the two statements fits as a punchline.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Judge: "You have committed the most heinous crime of murdering your parents." Convict: "Yes sir. But I hope....." that the jury would look with pity into the case of an orphan. that the jury would not order the last surviving member to be killed.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: The teacher scolded a pupil thus: "When George Washington was your age, he had become a world famous General." Pupil: "But I don't want to become the President of USA, sir." "And when he was your age, he had become the President of USA."

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: The economics teacher asked: "Give me an example of Barter System." Student: "......" "Income tax, sir. It acts as an income of the Government." "Home tax, sir. We pay tax for living in our own houses."

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: "My youngest boy has trouble with eczema", the woman said. "Doing hours asked, 'Goodness! How did he get it?'" The woman replied: "......" "I don't know. Otherwise I would've cured it." "If 'He hasn't got it; he just can't spell it."

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Judge: "So, you admit that you drove over the victim in a loaded truck?" Defendant: "I do, Your Honour. But......" "I did not intend to kill him." "I swear I had no idea that the truck was loaded."

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Judge: "Are you guilty?" Prisoner: "......" "I haven't heard the evidence yet." "Who is not, Your Honour?"

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: A young boy was taking an examination for a job. The question was: "What is the distance between the sun and the earth?" He answered: "......" "It must be farther from earth than moon." "At least twice the distance between the sun and the moon."

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: "I remember having been seeing you in this very court for the last twenty years", said the Judge to the accused. "Yes, Your Honour", said the accused, "......" "My promotion is due for such a long period." "I never practice has not made me perfect."

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: I: "I were Adam. For then, nobody would say, "You heard that before." II: "I could've seen the best of both the worlds."

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Orator who just finished his speech thus: "I am sorry I have spoken so long. You see, I haven't got my watch with me." To this a voice rose from the audience, "......" "But there was a calendar behind you." "Next time you must bring your watch with you."

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: The Manager reported to the Chief Manager, "Sir, Abdul has completely lost his hearing. What do we do with him?" The Chief manager replied: "......" "Fire him. We cannot have deaf accountants in our office." "Do as you please with him; I am partially deaf myself."
